operator_instances.transformation_goal: Increase data complexity by introducing whitespace, blank lines, and mixed line endings in the m/z input string to test parsing robustness.
operator_instances.rationale: The seed input is minimal (three simple m/z values). Adding typical formatting issues (spaces, blank lines, carriage returns) ensures the solution can handle real-world messy input. Since the API expects one m/z per line, the parser must handle extra whitespace and empty lines gracefully.

exception_tests.description: Verify that no exception is raised when processing the variant input.

expected_failure_modes: Failure to parse lines with trailing/leading spaces
Failure to handle carriage return line endings

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Prove that the function correctly counts unique matching m/z values even when the input string contains extra whitespace, blank lines, and mixed line endings.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The function must parse each line as an m/z value after stripping whitespace and ignoring blank lines.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior may fail if it assumes exactly one m/z per line without trimming, or fails on blank lines.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Execute _get_num_matches with the whitespace-laden input string and compare output against a (precomputed) correct count from the same API call.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new requirement introduced by this operator.
